description:
Jira: TBD
Distilled from mini-plan (think-t2d). Spec dir: .spec/
Worktree: /Users/mz/src/specled_ex-streaming-attribution
Branch: specled_-0y8/streaming-attribution
Refined plan: /Users/mz/Documents/my-vault/LLMs/plans/-Users-mz-src-specled-ex/2026-07-02-streaming-attribution/runs/20260702-071642-claude-mini/05-refined-plan.md

Evidence-based per-cover-id attribution for merged tagged_tests verification runs: process-group kill on timeout (S1), streaming ExUnit formatter artifact + per-cover strength distribution with strict artifact-absent degradation (S2), resume pass over never-started covers (S3). ADR specled.decision.evidence_based_attribution authored at distill. External prerequisites specled_-s65.6 and specled_-s65.7 are CLOSED and merged into main (this branch bases off 643bc24 which contains both).

comments.text:
NOTE: main-branch drift detected during orchestration.

origin/main advanced 3 commits past this epic's fork point (643bc24) via
specled_-j4p (realization-baseline split), which deletes/moves decisions and
requirements this epic branch still carries: specled.decision.dedicated_realization_baseline,
specled.binding.hash_store_dedicated_file, specled.binding.hash_store_legacy_fallback,
specled.index.legacy_baseline_hoist, specled.index.state_fully_derived.

Effect: plain `mix spec.check` (default base=origin/main) now fails with
append_only/decision_deleted and append_only/requirement_deleted errors on
THIS epic branch, independent of any ticket's own diff — confirmed by running
it on the epic worktree with only S1 merged (before S2 landed).
`mix spec.check --base 643bc24` (the epic's true fork point) passes clean:
status=pass errors=0 warnings=0, same 3 INFO branch_guard_missing_subject_update
findings already attested by S1 and S2 via Spec-Drift trailers.

Orchestrator decision: for the remainder of this epic's merge gates and
verifier spawns, using `mix spec.check --base 643bc24` instead of the bare
project command, to avoid false FAILs from unrelated main drift. This is
NOT a scope expansion or a code change — purely an evaluation-base choice,
same category as the .spec/state.json generated-file handling.

Follow-up (deferred, not blocking orchestration): the epic branch needs a
rebase onto origin/main before the final PR, so plain `mix spec.check`
passes again and the PR diff is clean. Flagging for whoever runs /open-pr —
not doing it now mid-pipeline since it would rewrite already-merged S1/S2
commit SHAs and cascade into every stage branch.

title: Streaming per-test attribution for merged tagged_tests runs
